scenario.description: Discover the tail command and use it to inspect the end of the app log.
scenario.prompt: Use the runtime to find the right command and show the last three lines of /logs/app.log.

/accounts/acme.md.text:
Customer: Acme Corp
Status: renewal at risk
Owner: sara@example.com
Notes: prefers Monday check-ins and a concise status summary.

/drafts/qbr.md.text:
# QBR draft

Open items:
- Revenue variance
- Renewal risk

/logs/app.log.text:
2026-03-12T10:00:01Z INFO startup complete
2026-03-12T10:01:10Z ERROR payment timeout order=123
2026-03-12T10:01:11Z ERROR payment timeout order=124
2026-03-12T10:01:12Z WARN retry scheduled order=124
2026-03-12T10:02:10Z ERROR failed login user=alice
2026-03-12T10:03:55Z INFO healthcheck ok

/notes/todo.txt.text:
1. Review refund incident timeline
2. Confirm Acme renewal owner
3. Draft follow-up email

searchDocs.title: Refund timeout incident retro
searchDocs.body: Payment timeouts spiked during a deploy. Root cause was a retry storm against the order service.

searchDocs.title: Acme renewal risk notes
searchDocs.body: Acme wants a tighter weekly status update and is blocked on invoice mapping.

searchDocs.title: EU VAT invoice checklist
searchDocs.body: Collect legal entity name, billing address, VAT ID, and invoice delivery contact before issuing invoices.

searchDocs.title: Login failure playbook
searchDocs.body: Repeated failed login errors can indicate bot traffic or a stale SSO configuration.

fetchResources.text:runbook: Escalate payment timeouts to the checkout on-call if the error rate exceeds 2%.

memory: Refund incident touched payments, billing, and checkout.
Ops owner for production config is ops@example.com.

steps.output:
tail: Show the last N lines or bytes from stdin or a file.
Usage: tail [-n N|-c N|-N] [path]

Examples:
  tail -n 20 /logs/app.log
  tail -c 16 /notes/todo.txt
  cat /logs/app.log | tail -n 50

[exit:0 | 0ms]
